title: Free parties in France come under increasing surveillance
subhed: Under an upcoming bill, which is set to be debated in the Assemblée Nationale on Monday, prison sentences would apply not only to the organizers of these self-managed techno parties, but also to their attendees. Protest parties have taken place across France in response to this crackdown.

What are prediction markets, the new form of sports betting promoted by the football World Cup?
subhed: Online platforms known as prediction markets, a form of largely unregulated online betting, have seen rapid growth, fueled in particular by the surging popularity of sports betting.

title: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'France's top priority will be to regain control of its public and welfare finances'
subhed: Before stepping down, the governor of the Bank of France, in a letter to President Emmanuel Macron, listed France's shortcomings. Economist Jean Pisani-Ferry welcomes this approach in his column.

title: Mysterious whale 'necropolis' discovered at the bottom of the Indian Ocean
subhed: A team of researchers has uncovered hundreds of fossilized whale carcasses off the southwest coast of Australia. Spanning 1,200 kilometers, this is the largest, deepest and oldest cetacean bone graveyard ever observed.

title: Paris's Quai Branly Museum turns 20: A look back at its turbulent history
subhed: The Paris institution is celebrating its anniversary with great fanfare this weekend. But behind the festivities, the museum continues to face many challenges, particularly economic.

What is included in France's comprehensive law against gender-based and sexual violence?
subhed: Following the death of 11-year-old Lyhanna in early June, momentum is growing for a comprehensive law to protect women and children, combining prevention and prosecution measures.

title: The uncertain fate of non-Russian prisoners of war captured by Ukrainian forces
subhed: Hundreds of foreign fighters held in captivity in Ukraine find themselves in legal limbo, as Moscow refuses to include them in prisoner exchanges.

title: Does the decline in reading and the rise of screens signal the end of the democratic era?
subhed: While concerns about the weakening of the mind in the face of new technologies are nothing new, the digital revolution and artificial intelligence now point to a possible end of book civilization.
